name: All Campus Theatre
summary: All Campus Theatre is the University of Tennessee's undergraduate theatre association. We produce  several events and activities that offer the experience of theatre to students, including full productions, original works, and more. Everyone is welcome.

name: American Institute of Chemical Engineers
summary: American Institute of  Chemical Engineers (AIChE)

summary: American Institute of Aeronautics & Astronautics (AIAA) is an international professional based organization rooted in the Aerospace industry. We hold general body meetings with industry speakers, social events, and industry trips.

name: American Nuclear Society
summary:
Student Section of the American Nuclear Society

Working to promote nuclear science and connect our members with research and career opportunities. Students outside of Nuclear Engineering are welcome!

name: American Society of Agricultural and Biological Engineers
summary: American Society of Agricultural and Biological Engineers (ASABE)

name: American Society of Civil Engineers
summary: Founded in 1852, the American Society of Civil Engineers (ASCE) represents more than 140,000 members of the civil engineering profession worldwide and is America's oldest national engineering society, consisting of student and professional chapters.

name: Volunteers in Earth and Water
summary: We are an interdisciplinary club focused on assisting Knoxville in all things water and the environment. We specialize in hosting lunch-and-learns in which professional speakers present on topics relating to civil engineering and water resources.

name: Bachelor's Social Work Student Organization
summary: Bachelor's Social Work Student Organization (BSWO)

name: Beta Theta Pi
summary: Beta Theta Pi, a Social IFC fraternity, which strives to uphold the values of Aid and Mutual Assistance, Cultivation of Intellect, Trust, Responsibility, and Integrity. We strive to uphold the values we were founded on in 1839. http://www.betathetapi.org

name: Black Cultural Programming Committee
summary: The Black Cultural Programming Committee develop and present programs and activities that enhance the University community’s awareness of African-American culture & its contributions to our society.

name: Black Educators of Tomorrow
summary: Black Educators of Tomorrow. We are a trailblazing group of young, future Educators who want to increase awareness of issues related to diversity and its influence on the teaching profession. If you believe in the power of education, we are for you!

name: Women's Coordinating Council
summary: The Women's Coordinating Council is a student-led organization that prioritizes programming and activism and seeks to empower folks from all gender, ethnic, social, and economic backgrounds using an intersectional feminist lens.

name: Chi Epsilon, The Civil Engineering Honors Society
summary: Chi Epsilon is a Civil Engineering Honors Society for civil engineering students who are in the top third of their sophomore, junior, or senior class. These students must also embody the 4 pillars of character, scholarship, sociability, and practicality

name: Chi Omega
summary: Chi Omegas are well balanced women who are involved on their campuses and in their communities. As a prominent national women's fraternity, Chi Omega provides countless opportunities for fun and friendship during college and beyond.

name: Clement Hall Residents' Association
summary: CHRA is a body of student representatives for Frank G. Clement Residence Hall. Any resident of Clement Hall is automatically a member of CHRA. CHRA strives to provide programs and opportunities for residents to engage with one another and our campus.

name: Theta Tau
summary: Theta Tau: The University of Tennessee chapter of Theta Tau Co-ed Professional Engineering Fraternity.

summary: An organization for undergrads interested in learning about and connecting with the anthropology community at UTK!

name: National Residence Hall Honorary
summary: NRHH is an organization dedicated to recognition and service, specifically for students living on campus in our residence halls. We set up service projects and foster connections with other dedicated members.

name: National Society of Black Engineers
summary:
Mission: 

To increase the number of culturally responsible Black engineers who excel academically, succeed professionally and positively impact the community.

name: Tennessee Club Baseball
summary: Official Club Baseball Team of University of Tennessee, Knoxville.

summary: Official student organization for undergraduate Geography and Sustainability majors/minors within the Department of Geography. As a group, we provide resources for unification and development to all majors in the Department.

summary: Organization for members of the Republican Party on campus. We host speakers, foster political debate and insight, and provide volunteer/job opportunities in local politics.

summary: To complement the socialization of masters level student affairs in the College Student Personnel (CSP) program at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville through educational, professional, and social development opportunities.

summary: Entrepreneurs Club is a place for student entrepreneurs to gather and discuss business ideas, meet local successful business owners, learn about what it takes to be an entrepreneur and to feel supported by their community!

The official UTK Anime-Manga club.

summary: We are the official Men's Ultimate Frisbee club team at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville.  Contact TennMUltimate@gmail.com or follow our IG @tennesseeultimate for information on joining.

summary: This is a club dedicated to helping undergraduates who are in the field of microbiology. Whether future plans include professional or graduate programs, we are here to help you explore all possibilities.

summary: Kappa Alpha Pi Pre-Law and Government Co-ed Professionally Fraternity at the University of Tennessee

summary: Platypus organizes reading groups, public fora, research, and journalism focused on problems and tasks inherited from the “Old” (1920s-30s), “New” (1960s-70s), and post-political (1980s-90s) Left for possibilities of emancipatory politics today.

summary: Public Relations Student Society of America

summary: Reformed University Fellowship

summary: Sigma Alpha Epsilon strives to give young men the leadership, scholarship, service and social experiences they need to excel in the walls outside their campus and once they graduate. We firmly believe membership is for life.

summary: Diverse Organization of Business Students at The University of Tennessee

summary: Earth, Environmental, and Planetary Sciences Student Association (Geo Club) promotes fellowship among Earth, Environmental and Planetary Science Students through community building events and local geoscience outreach.

summary: The Tennessee Undergraduate Economics Society is an academic organization which strives to enhance the knowledge of students interested in or studying economics.

name: Habitat for Humanity Campus Chapter
summary:
The Habitat for Humanity Campus Chapter supports Habitat for Humanity through volunteer work, fundraising, and spreading awareness about Habitat's mission.

name: Sigma Kappa
summary: Sigma Kappa, Alpha Delta chapter

summary: The SAA-UTK serves as an organization to connect students with an interest in archival practices and to facilitate learning and networking within the profession.

name: Business Analytics Society
summary: The Business Analytics Society serves as UT's main platform for all students interested in the growing field of Business Analytics and data analysis.

name: Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers at UTK
summary: SHPE changes lives by empowering the Hispanic community to realize their fullest potential and impacts the world through STEM awareness, access, support, and development.

name: Society of Physics Students
summary: Professional development and resources for students interested in physics.

name: Society of Women Engineers at University of Tennessee
summary: Empower women to achieve full potential in careers as engineers and leaders, expand the image of the engineering and technology professions as a positive force in improving the quality of life, and demonstrate the value of diversity and inclusion.

name: South Carrick Residents Association
summary: South Carrick Residents Association

name: Student Alumni Associates
summary: Student Alumni Associates (SAA) is a organization that aims to strengthen bonds between UT students and alumni. Members are campus leaders who represent UT at philanthropy and alumni events.

name: Women in Nuclear (University of Tennessee Chapter)
summary: The UT Chapter of Women in Nuclear is part of a national organization that focuses on professional development and supporting cultures in which women and men in nuclear energy and technology fields can succeed.

name: American Library Association, University of Tennessee, Knoxville Chapter
summary: ALA-UTK is the University of Tennessee, Knoxville student chapter of the American Libraries Association parent organization. We encourage participation in students’ state library associations, as well as broader national events and relationships.

name: Poultry Science Club
summary: Poultry Science Club is an organization that participates in activities aimed at promoting the poultry industry.

name: Alpha Kappa Psi
summary: Alpha Kappa Psi is a Co-ed Business Fraternity founded in 1904 with the purpose of developing its members into principled business leaders.

name: Environmental Law Organization
summary: The Environmental Law Organization seeks to connect its members to each other and the broader environmental law community and to inform them of the environmental law and policy issues they are likely to encounter during their careers.

name: International Business Club
summary: We offer social and professional networking between students and executives in a wide variety of roles who are passionate about issues surrounding international business and diplomacy.

name: Tabletop Gaming Club at UTK
summary: The Tabletop Gaming Club at UTK seeks to allow people to enjoy card, board, and role-playing games of various types among friends. They can learn from experienced veterans or compete in tournaments, or simply enjoy casual play.

name: Volunteer Short Films
summary: Volunteer Short Films is a community of filmmakers and creatives who aid one another in content creation. Our goal is to create films and have fun while doing it. All experience levels are welcome!

name: Student Council for Exceptional Children
summary: The Council for Exceptional Children (CEC) is the largest international professional organization dedicated to improving the educational success of individuals with disabilities and/or gifts and talents. SCEC is the student chapter of CEC.

name: CLiP - The Portuguese Language Club
summary: Clube da Língua Portuguesa

name: Entomology and Plant Pathology Graduate Student Association
summary: The Entomology and Plant Pathology Graduate Student Association is a student organization focused creating a positive environment and dialogue between students, community outreach events, and presenting research in our field to parties that may benefit.

name: Reese Hall Residents Association
summary: Reese Hall is a residence hall located in the Presidential Complex and home to 514 male freshman and upperclassmen students. The Reese Hall Resident Association exists to advocate and provide programs for the residents of Reese Hall.

name: Global Studies Student Association
summary: The mission of the Global Studies Student Association is to foster a community of globally minded students by providing opportunities for academic enrichment, professional development, and cross-cultural engagement.

name: Construction Science Club
summary: The C.S. Club is a student-governed organization, which provides engagement with the construction industry. Extracurricular activities incorporate introduction to professional societies, volunteerism, seminars, trips and fellowship.

name: Systers: Women in EECS @ UTK
summary: The mission of Systers is to recruit, mentor, and retain women in the fields of Computer Engineering, Computer Science, and Electrical Engineering.

name: Pre-Physician Assistant Student Association
summary: PASA is an organization dedicated to students interested in becoming a PA. Our meetings focus on necessary steps to becoming a PA by offering opportunities to learn about the profession, prepare oneself for applying, & engage in community service work.

name: Center for Career Development & Academic Exploration
summary: We provide a wide array of career-related services for all students. View our full description for more details.

name: Darwin Day UT
summary: The goal of Darwin Day UT is to promote evolutionary science education while fostering an interest in science in both the student body and the Knoxville community through lecture series, field trips, and other activities.

name: Multicultural Graduate Student Organization
summary:
The Multicultural Graduate Student Organization (MGSO) is a student-run organization that focuses on promoting diversity, equity, and inclusion within the graduate student community at UT.

For recent updates, follow our Instagram page: @mgso.utk

name: Interfraternity Council
summary: The Interfraternity Council represents 21 fraternities at the University of Tennessee. Our community offers chapters of 15 to 225 men with 17 housed groups. Through philanthropic events and community service, the IFC strives to build the best men.

name: Pi Sigma Alpha Honor Society
summary: Pi Sigma Alpha is a nationally recognized Political Science Honor Society.

name: Office of Sustainability
summary: The Office of Sustainability works to coordinate, manage, advise and report on sustainable initiatives at UT, through education, engagement, research, data collection and collaborations with a variety of on-campus and community stakeholders.

name: Delta Kappa Epsilon
summary: Founded in 1844 at Yale University, DKE is one of the oldest collegiate fraternities in the world. A Deke is described as "equal parts gentleman, scholar, and jolly good fellow".

name: Plant Sciences Club at The University of Tennssee
summary: The Plant Sciences Club at The University of Tennessee will promote the exchange of experience, knowledge and ideas about plants between students of all majors.

name: Black Student Union at University of Tennessee
summary: The goal of the Black Student Union is to unify black and minority students on the campus of UTK. Through dialogues, programming, and service, we will create a supportive and inclusive environment.

name: Out in Science, Technology, Engineering, & Mathematics at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville
summary: Out in Science, Technology, Engineering & Mathematics at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville (oSTEM at UTK) is the campus chapter of the national oSTEM organization, dedicated to supporting LGBTQ+ students in STEM and their allies.

name: HackUTK
summary: The mission of HackUTK is to promote student interest in the field of computer and network security through participation in and sponsorship of capture the flag competitions and related activities that inspire, develop, and empower a future generation

name: Ad Club
summary: The purpose of Ad Club is to unite + inspire + encourage + guide students of any major with interests in advertising, marketing, or general creative business practices.

name: VOLT
summary: The University of Tennessee's premiere Co-Ed a cappella group!

name: Gender Minorities and Women in Physics
summary: The Gender Minorities and Women in Physics (GMaWiP) organization at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville is a student led organization that was created to bring together those that wish to empower and support gender minorities in physics.

name: Eta Theta Chapter of Phi Sigma Pi National Honor Fraternity
summary: Phi Sigma Pi is a gender inclusive national honor Fraternity, with the goal of bringing the best and brightest students together to make a larger impact in their campus, community and world.

name: SACNAS Chapter at The University of Tennessee, Knoxville
summary: To further the work of, to improve the effectiveness of, and to enhance the public understanding of and appreciation for Chicanos, Latinos, Native Americans, Alaska Natives, Native Hawaiians, and other underrepresented minorities in the sciences.

name: Big Orange Consulting
summary: Big Orange Consulting's mission is to foster connections between the local community, alumni consultants, and the University of Tennessee MBA student population as well as to prepare students for case interviews. This is a graduate level club.

name: Material Advantage - UTK
summary: Professional student organization for materials science and engineering. Chapter hosts and participates in a variety of materials science and engineering related activities, events, and competitions.

name: Theodorus Chapter of Alpha Rho Chi
summary: Alpha Rho Chi is a professional and social fraternity of Architecture and the allied arts. We aim to create connection between students in the arts as well as practicing professionals.

name: Black Law Students Association
summary: The Marilyn Yarbrough Chapter of the University of Tennessee, a member of the Southern region, effects NBLSA's goals through a variety of initiatives, including academic and professional workshops, mentoring programs, and additional events.

name: Advocates For Autism
summary: Advocates For Autism aims to raise awareness and educate the campus about Autism and provide an inclusive environment for autistic students as well as serve as allies.

name: Women of Business Analytics at Tennessee
summary: Women of Business Analytics at Tennessee (WomBAT) provides professional development and networking opportunities for women (undergrad & grad) interested in pursuing careers in analytics.

name: Best Buddies UTK
summary: Best Buddies UTK works directly with the Future program to create one-to-one friendships between people with and without intellectual and developmental disabilities.

name: Association for Information Science and Technology
summary: ASIS&T is a multidisciplinary organization with members from fields such as librarianship, computer science, linguistics, management, engineering, law, medicine,and education. It is interested in bridging the gap between information research and practice

name: Pre-Optometry Club at The University of Tennessee
summary: The purpose of the Pre-Optometry Club at The University of Tennessee is to focus on bringing students around the campus together who are interested in seeking the optometry profession to discuss topics to help students with the admission process.

name: The Astronomy Club
summary: Enthusiastic astronomy lovers who enjoy looking/learning about the night sky through interactions with telescopes, planetarium shows, and other science activities!

name: Investment Group at the University of Tennessee
summary: The University of Tennessee Investment Group's mission is to develop a better understanding of financial markets through trading, investing, and networking with industry professionals.

name: LEAD at the University of Tennessee
summary: UT LEAD

name: Management Society
summary: The Management Society is a 100% student-led organization at Haslam College of Business at the University of Tennessee working to empower students to lead meaningful, impactful professional lives.

name: Pursuit - The Undergraduate Journal of Research at the University of Tennessee
summary: Pursuit is the first scholarly peer-reviewed journal at UT Knoxville managed and edited by undergraduates and devoted specifically to their research achievements.

name: Anthropology Graduate Student Association
summary: AGSA holds meetings to facilitate communication within the Department of Anthropology graduate students;  hosts annual events to promote social, research, and academic camaraderie; and promotes interest in Anthropology within UT as well as the public.

name: Financial Management Association
summary: The Financial Management Association hosts monthly business speakers, and active members are eligible to take trips to New York, among other potential locations.

name: National Organization of Minority Architecture Students
summary: As the National Organization of minority architecture students, we strive to create a safe space where design students can come together and have their voices heard regardless of race, gender, sexuailty, etc.

name: Arnold Air Society
summary: The primary mission of Arnold Air Society is to build and develop leaders and officers for the United States Air Force by participating in service projects and professional development workshops.

name: GAYmerz
summary: A group made to provide people of all sexual orientations and gender identities a safe environment to play and discuss video/board games amongst others in the community. Always accepting new members, just stop by!

name: American Institute of Architecture Students University of Tennessee Chapter
summary:
The American Institute of Architecture Students (AIAS) is national independent, nonprofit, student-run organization dedicated to advancing leadership, design, and service among architecture students.
(From aias.org)

name: Service Learning and Outreach with 3D Printers for Engineers
summary: This group focuses on STEM exposure to the local Knoxville community by 3d printing at the East Tennessee Children's Hospital as well as working with elementary educational programs, and other outreach events.

name: Miniatures Gaming Club at UT
summary: The University of Tennessee Miniatures Gaming Club is dedicated to the organization of miniature gaming on campus. We support Warhammer 40k, Age of Sigmar, Infinity, Battletech, and anything else involving miniatures.

name: Undergraduate Association of Forensic Sciences
summary: UAFS is an interdisciplinary club that helps those interested in forensics connect with likeminded individuals and future forensic professionals gain networking connections and participate in professional development activities.

name: Pro Bono at UT
summary: Pro Bono at UT is a community service organization with significant student leadership and staff direction at the University of Tennessee College of Law.  The program strives to connect law students with area attorneys providing pro bono service to clien

name: Graduate Physics Society
summary:
The Graduate Physics Society (GPS) is a community of graduate students with an interest in physics.
The mission of GPS is to provide professional development.

name: Engineers in Medicine Society
summary: EMED is a pre-health engineering society dedicated to providing resources and opportunities to engineering majors in pursuit of professional school.

name: Microbiology Graduate Student Association
summary: The Micro GSA aims to promote professional development and personal advancement of its members through academic events, social gatherings, and networking opportunities with the larger Microbiology community.

name: Coalition of Black Social Workers
summary: The CBSW exists to engage, connect, and empower Black social work students and professionals in the community, with the goal of increasing awareness about issues specific to the Black community and cultivating a network of support.

name: Relay for Life at UTK
summary: Relay for Life is the largest non-profit fundraiser in the world to raise money for cancer. Organized by volunteers, over 4-million people come together in this event to raise money for cancer, celebrate our survivors, and remember those we have lost.

name: Women of Haslam
summary: Women of Haslam is a business society at The University of Tennessee that supports and inspires women to develop professionally and personally.

name: Office of Student Media
summary: The Office of Student Media serves as the launching point for student journalists and those interested in the media field. Open to all majors and disciplines, the Office of Student Media offers opportunities for any student on campus.

name: Political Science Graduate Student Association
summary: PSGSA is an organization for Political Science graduate students at the University of Tennessee that seeks to facilitate collaboration between students and faculty and works to advance the personal and professional goals of the students it represents.

name: Student Support Services
summary: Student Support Services

name: Nuclear Engineering Graduate Student Assembly
summary: The mission of the Nuclear Engineering Graduate Student Assembly is to serve as a collaborative forum that enables University of Tennessee graduate students in the Nuclear Engineering Department to initiate and engage in discussions, events, and programs

name: Leading Women of Tomorrow UTK
summary: LWT is a student-led organization at the University of Tennessee focused on professional and personal development of young women. Our organization aims to encourage and support women to pursue leadership positions and connect them with women leaders.

name: Engineers Without Borders at the University of Tennessee
summary: Engineers Without Borders builds a better world through engineering projects that empower communities to meet their basic human needs. As UTK volunteers, we work with communities to find appropriate solutions for their infrastructure needs.

name: Supply Chain Analytics and Reporting
summary: A student organization dedicated to building scaled components of a supply chain using robotics/automation, coding and applying supply chain principles like collaboration and data collection for process improvements.

name: Ablaze Magazine
summary:
Ablaze Magazine is a student-powered magazine publication that covers women’s
topics and feminist issues.

Formerly known as Honey Magazine.

name: Thrive College Ministry
summary: TCM is an organization aimed to connect students with the Thrive program

name: Turning Point USA
summary: Turning Point USA is a 501(c)3 non-profit organization whose mission is to educate students about the importance of fiscal responsibility, free markets, and capitalism through innovative campus activism and non-partisan, thought-provoking discussion.

name: Vietnamese Student Association
summary: Vietnamese Student Association exists to help educate and to bring awareness to Vietnamese culture, while pushing for inclusivity & diversity. Our purpose is to provide a safe space for students to explore & embrace Vietnamese tradition and culture.

name: Business Honors Council
summary: We are dedicated to enhancing the academic, professional, philanthropic, ad social endeavors of honors students in the Haslam College of Business. Members of the advisory board will organize events each semester relevant to honors students in HCB.

name: Volunteer Team
summary: Volunteer Team meets with and provides important information to prospective student athletes & their families. Team members have the opportunity to share their knowledge of UT and its great traditions, including the outstanding varsity athletic programs.

name: Brain Exercise Initiative at The University of Tennessee, Knoxville Chapter
summary: The Brain Exercise Initiative is a nonprofit organization that uses simple math, writing, and reading aloud exercised as an intervention to improve cognitive function in those with Alzheimer's Disease.

name: Plant Sciences Graduate Student Association
summary: An organization to unite and engage graduate students in Plant Sciences through social, educational, professional, and service activities.

name: Cycling Club at UT
summary: We ride all kinds of bikes.

name: The Real Estate Club
summary: The Real Estate Club welcomes all students that are or may be interested in the area of Real Estate. We provide educational opportunities in all fields, including but not limited to commercial, industrial, and residential real estate.

name: National Student Speech Language and Hearing Association, UT Chapter
summary: NSSLHA serves as the unifying student organization on campus for Audiology and Speech Pathology students at the undergraduate and graduate level.

name: Phi Alpha Delta
summary: Phi Alpha Delta Law Fraternity strives to be the premier legal professional organization with a diverse membership that cultivates leaders and serves as a voice for the role of legal professionalism in our society.

name: 4FirstGen
summary: An organization to encourage and celebrate first-generation students who are taking the first step to success and allow an organization for opportunity, accomplishment, and support for those students who are striving for a higher standard of education.

name: Knights of the Hill
summary: Knights of the Hill is a Fantasy and Tabletop Gaming club.

name: Alpha Alpha Alpha Honor Society
summary: The mission of the Tri-Alpha, Gamma Upsilon chapter, is to assist in the academic, social, and professional growth of first-generation college students at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville.

name: The Camp Koinonia Organization
summary: Camp Koinonia is a week-long, overnight camp experience for youth and young adults with various disabilities, aged 7 through high school graduation. This program is completely student driven with roughly 100 UT students staffing the camp.

name: The Korean Graduate Student Association
summary: The Korean Graduate Student Association is established to provide a path for UT students with common interests to broaden the awareness and understanding of the Korean culture and deliver the Tennessee culture to Korea.

name: Engineering Fundamentals Program
summary: In the success-oriented environment of engage™️, students learn by interacting with others to create solutions to engineering problems.

name: Professional Sales Club
summary: Empowering the next generation of diverse sales talent through career/professional development and high-value professional engagement with an emphasis on creating a culture that prepares students for success and placement in professional sales careers.

name: Collegiate DECA at the University of Tennessee
summary: DECA aims to prepare emerging leaders and entrepreneurs in marketing, finance, hospitality and management in high schools and colleges around the globe.

name: Social Work PhD Student Organization
summary: The purpose of the Social Work PhD Student Organization (SWPHDSO) is to serve as a student organization specifically for social work PhD students.

name: Photography Club
summary: A creative outlet and learning community for photographers.

name: Sustainable Business Organization at the University of Tennessee
summary: We work to change the way UT does business in a more environmentally friendly way. With minimal sustainable initiatives in place at UT, we want to be a student-led organization whose goal is to raise student concern on the topic and establish change.

name: Haslam College of Business
summary: Register for events happening in the Haslam College of Business. Events are aimed at helping students connect with faculty and staff, learn about opportunities, and make the most of your academic endeavors in the Haslam College.

name: Biosystems engineering and soil science graduate students
summary: Social and professional development organization for BESS grad students

name: Latino Law Student Association
summary: Raise awareness and prepare members to face issues related to Latino/a’s participation in the judicial system; Promote career and networking opportunities for members within the legal profession; and Coordinate activities within the university community

name: Student Association of Project Management
summary: The purpose is to further understand the principles and practices of project management in any field, to network and connect with students and professionals to further education or future develop opportunities in the project management division.

name: PsychVOLogy
summary: We provide a community for students with an interest in psychology by organizing social activities, learning experiences, and career development opportunities.

name: Women in Engineering Program
summary: The Women in Engineering Program provides young girls and women with connections to resources, support, mentorship, and engaging programs designed for them to discover the passions that will guide their academic and professional careers.

name: The History Club
summary:
The History Club aims to promote community within the history department of UT,
to create a space to discuss historical topics and opportunities within the field, and
to connect students with professionals and faculty within the discipline.

name: Society of Women Engineers Graduate Group
summary: The mission of UT GradSWE is to provide graduate students who wish to participate in the Society of Women Engineers at the local level the support, resources, information, and financial support to do so.

name: Collegiate National Association for Music Education
summary: A professional organization unifying music education students by hosting professional development seminars and coordinating visits to conferences around the US.

name: Association of Latino Professionals for America
summary: The Association of Latino Professionals for America hopes to bring together students shared a goal of providing the resources to excel as hispanic business professionals and mold generations of successful leaders.

name: National Society of Black Women In Medicine
summary: The National Society of Black Women In Medicine is dedicated to increasing the retention of underrepresented minority women pursuing broad careers in healthcare and basic science.

name: The Information Sciences Club
summary: The Information Sciences Club provides students with a community for professional growth through networking, portfolio development, and sharing applications of information science with other entities within the University of Tennessee and Knoxville area.

name: Latino Business Student Association
summary: The Latino Business Student Association is a professional and social organization that aims to provide a community for Latinx students in the Haslam College of Business through workshops, guest speakers, networking events and social gatherings.

name: Asian Business Club
summary: The Asian Business Club provides a platform for students interested in Asian business, culture, and commerce to network, learn, and share experiences, often organizing events and opportunities for members to participate in.

name: Office of Title IX
summary: The Office of Title IX provides a comprehensive community approach to the institution’s Title IX responsibility to create and maintain a safe  environment for all students free from Sexual Harassment, Sexual Exploitation, and Retaliation.

name: IA Collaborative: IIDA+ASID Student Chapter at UTK
summary: The mission of the IA Collaborative: IIDA+ASID Student Chapter at UTK is to provide an environment for interior architecture students at the University of Tennessee to benefit from professional development and an exchange of knowledge and support.

name: Haslam Women of Color
summary: A student organization that aims provide a space for women of color in the Haslam College of Business and students interested in business to be able to network, build a sense of community, and strengthen their educational and professional goals.

name: Undergraduate International Law Club
summary: The Undergraduate International Law Club provides undergraduate students with an interest in international law with opportunities to engage with and learn about the field from those who practice, study, and teach it.

name: Center for Global Engagement
summary: Reporting directly to the Office of the Provost, the Center for Global Engagement works to lead, coordinate, and support the university’s strategies for global education, research, and engagement.

name: Vol Study Center
summary: The Vol Study Center offers study space, free printing and snacks/refreshments, and study sessions located across campus.

name: Future Minority Leaders of Justice
summary: Future Minority Leaders of Justice is a student-led organization aimed at attracting minorities who would like to attend law school or pursue a career within the realm of politics or government relation.

name: Privacy, Intellectual Property, and Technology Law
summary: The PIT is a student-led organization that aims to inform, educate, and expose students who are or may be interested in Privacy, Intellectual Property, or Technology Law by providing lectures, mentorships, and networking opportunities.

name: The Beauty In Marketing Group
summary: Beauty in Marketing Group fosters a vibrant, inclusive community at The University of Tennessee, Knoxville. Dedicated to marketing and PR in the beauty industry, we provide a safe space for collaboration, networking, and professional growth.

name: Women In Construction
summary: Primarily for construction science majors, a group for women in construction to meet, make real-world industry connections, and work with likeminded women preparing to work in the construction industry

name: NOBCChE Student Chapter at The University of Tennessee, Knoxville
summary: The University of Tennessee Knoxville NOBCChE Student Chapter is an organization dedicated to uniting aspiring and accomplished minds in chemistry and chemical engineering throughout the university and the greater Knoxville area.

name: Rocky Top Recovery
summary: Rocky Top Recovery is a collegiate recovery program at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville, dedicated to supporting Volunteers in recovery from substance use and mental health disorders.

name: Asian Pacific American Law Student Association
summary: APALSA of UT is committed to advocating for Asian-Pacific American interests at the College of Law, promoting career and networking opportunities for its members, and facilitating allyship within the student body.

name: Diverse Organization Propelling Excellence at Haslam
summary: Diverse Organization Propelling Excellence (DOPE) cultivates growth for BIPOC business graduate students in Haslam through job tours, speakers, conferences, fostering academic, professional, and personal success.

name: The Advocates' Guild
summary: A professional group of aspiring legal professionals that will learn from each other through activities like listening to lawyers, working through cases, research, and other professional development activities.

name: Alpha Gamma Delta
summary: Established in 1904, Alpha Gamma Delta is committed to providing an experience grounded in sincerity, cherished friendships and high ideals.

name: Type 1 Vols
summary: The purpose of “Type One Vols” is to educate, advocate, and have the support of university allies to better prepare students as they navigate living with diabetes on a college campus, while creating service and philanthropic endeavors for all members.

name: Law Students for Life
summary: The mission (or purpose) of the “Organization’s Name” is to Members of (Your School) Students for Life work to save lives threatened by induced abortion, euthanasia, and the destruction of human embryos for research. In furtherance of these goals, membe

name: Bredesen Center Student Advocacy Council
summary: The Student Advocacy Council (SAC) meets periodically with the Bredesen Center staff. A primary goal of the Bredesen Center is to help students feel at home

name: Honors Student Engagement Council
summary: Honors Student Engagement Council is a UH student-driven initiative that is open to all University Honors students. Each semester HSEC coordinates inclusive, innovative events and service opportunities for University Honors students to build community.

name: GeoguessrUTK
summary: Geoguessr is a beneficial, unique, and immersive tool to learning about different countries’ culture, architecture, language, and terrain. The organization will help students learn to geolocate in google street view using context clues around the world

name: The Pulsera Project
summary: The Pulsera Project is a nonprofit that educates, empowers, and connects Central American artists with students in more than 3,600 U.S. schools through the sale of colorful handwoven bracelets, or "pulseras" in Spanish.

name: Trumpets of the Rocky Top
summary: Trumpets of Rocky Top is an organization for the members of the University of Tennessee Trumpet Ensemble. The mission of this organization is to provide diverse and educational musical performances to the University of Tennessee and beyond

name: Healthy Celebrations UTK
summary:
Healthy Celebrations is an organization that builds a community where college students can
socialize and run in an encouraging and safe environment.

name: Student Philanthropy and Preservation Society of Knoxville
summary: SPPS explores key historical topics in East Tennessee, such as cultural heritage, social progress, economic development, and natural history. By combining service with education, we highlight the importance of preserving history and passing it on.

name: Tri-Star Wing Booster Club
summary:
The mission of the Tri-Star Wing Booster Club is to support the cadets of the Tri-Star Wing in their
leadership development and academic pursuits.

name: Lambda Pi Eta
summary: UTK's Alpha Gamma chapter of LPE is the National Communication Association’s official honor society.

name: Students Supporting Israel at The University of Tennessee
summary: Students Supporting Israel at The University of Tennessee is established to provide students with an engagement forum for pro-Israel advocacy.

name: Energy and Natural Resource Law Society
summary: Affinity group for students in the College of Law interested in practicing law in energy, natural resource law, or conservation or are personally interested in related issues.

name: First Generation Haslam Students
summary: The purpose of First Generation Haslam Students is to provide support, resources and community for first-generation students enrolled in Haslam College of Business.

name: Vols for Reproductive Justice
summary: Our club is dedicated to advancing the principles of reproductive justice. We strive to create a supportive and inclusive community where members can engage in advocacy, education, and activism surrounding reproductive rights, health, and autonomy.

name: Graduate Researchers in Ecology, Behavior, and Evolution
summary: An EEB graduate student organization that coordinates events, organizes professional development activities, communicates with faculty, and focuses on improving student well-being.

name: Engineering Youth Education
summary: The mission of the Engineering Youth Education organization is to help the youth understand the different disciplines of engineering and show prospective engineers the options they have in the future.

name: West Tennessee Graduate Student Association (WTGSA)
summary: The West Tennessee Graduate Student Association (WTGSA) is an interdisciplinary student organization for individuals who work or study at the West Tennessee AgResearch and Education Center in any capacity while completing their degree.

name: Global Health Scholars
summary: The purpose of Global Health Scholars Club is to promote interest in the field of biology of/in global health-related topics including research, and to network like-minded students, faculty, and other professionals

name: Society of Manufacturing Engineers
summary: The mission of the Society of Manufacturing Engineers is to accelerate the widespread adoption of manufacturing technologies and build talents and capabilities.

name: World Languages and Cultures Graduate Student Association
summary: The vision of the WLC-GSA is to develop a strong Graduate Student Association that will benefit students before, during, and after their studies at the University of Tennessee and to further showcase and promote the excellence of the Department.

name: Ukrainian Club
summary: The Ukrainian Club celebrates Ukrainian culture, traditions, and history on campus through events, workshops, and discussions, collaborating with local nonprofit koloHUB and local businesses. All students are welcome to join and engage!

name: NAMI On Campus at The University of Tennessee Knoxville (NOC UTK)
summary: NAMI On Campus at The University of Tennessee, Knoxville (NOC UTK) is a student-led organization affiliated with the National Alliance on Mental Illness (NAMI) that promotes mental health awareness, education, and advocacy within the UTK community.

name: Law and Politics Club
summary: This would be an organization where students could learn about law applications, processes, and make connections. Members are encouraged to explore the law field and meet fellow students interested in law and politics.

name: Pre-Law Pathways
summary: Pre-Law Pathways at UTK is a student organization offering exposure to various legal fields, helping students explore their interests and strengthen their law school applications through events, workshops, and networking.

name: Rocky Top Student Veterans of America
summary: The Rocky Top Student Veterans of America (SVA) supports military veterans at UTK by fostering community, providing resources, and advocating for their success in higher education. The group promotes camaraderie, leadership, and veteran awareness at UTK

name: Volunteer's Theme Park Engineering Group
summary: We are a club for students to explore the themed entertainment industry through presentations, tours, competitions, and trips. We connect, learn, and grow through hands-on experiences, industry insights, and a shared passion for amusement design.

name: Book To Build
summary: Book to Build is a STEM project organization at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville that bridges classroom theory and real world engineering by turning ideas into working prototypes. BTB gives students the opportunity to design, build, and test ideas!

name: CSI Student Collaboration @ University of Tennessee, Knoxville
summary: CSI Student Collaboration at the University of Tennessee, Knoxville, is an organization affiliated with the local CSI Knoxville Chapter.

name: Computational Biology and Sciences Club
summary: An organization that brings together students interested in the computational sciences. Meetings will consist of discussions of interesting papers, guest lecturers, presentations, and more.

name: Haslam Business Law Society
summary: The Haslam Business Law Society is a student-led organization dedicated to exploring the intersection of business, law, and policy. Through speaker events and networking opportunities, we prepare members for careers in business law and related fields.

name: The Horological Society
summary: The Horological Society is dedicated to the study of timekeeping and watches, blending engineering, design, and history while fostering community through workshops and events.

name: Percussion at UTK
summary: The mission of Percussion at UTK is to inspire, educate, and support student percussionists by providing opportunities for personal and professional growth in the field of percussion.

name: American Constitution Society at the Winston College of Law
summary: ACS is a progressive constitutional law student organization.

name: Vol of Duty
summary: Vol of Duty is a literary gaming website for the University of Tennessee, Knoxville, aimed at providing an opportunity for students to publish articles and participate in a community that analyzes and discusses all forms of videogames.

name: SGI Vols Buddhists Club
summary: SGI Vols Buddhists club seeks to contribute to peace, culture and education based on the philosophy and ideals of the Buddhism of Nichiren Daishonin as shared by the Soka Gakkai International.

name: Students of East Africa
summary: Students of East Africa (S.E.A.) at UTK unites students from East African backgrounds, fosters cultural awareness, promotes service in the Knoxville community, and creates an inclusive space for social and professional connections.

name: Ghana Students Association-UTK
summary: The Ghana Students’ Association at UTK supports Ghanaian students, fosters academic and professional growth, promotes culture, and builds connections within the university and local community.

name: Student Association of Latinos United for the Development in Health
summary: SALUD empowers Latino and first-generation students pursuing healthcare careers through mentorship, resources, and professional development, while also serving the Knoxville Latino community to promote equitable, accessible healthcare.

name: Phi Alpha Delta Searcy Chapter
summary: Phi Alpha Delta Law Fraternity, International strives to be the premier legal professional organization with a diverse membership that cultivates leaders and serves as a voice for the role of legal professionalism in our society.

name: Black Musicians Alliance!
summary: The Black Musicians Alliance is dedicated to empowering and uplifting all minority artists across all musical genres. We strive to foster a supportive community that celebrates cultural heritage, advocates for equity and representation.

name: SheThrives
summary: SheThrives is an organization that promotes women's health and safety in a variety of different ways through service, communication with peers, and activities. It is a supportive group of women who want to empower themselves and others to speak up.

name: Neuroscience, Psychology, and Law Association
summary: The Neuroscience, Psychology, and Law Association (NPLA) explores how neuroscience and psychology, which study the mind, brain, and nervous system, intersect with the law.

name: Promoting Electric Propulsion Boat Club
summary: The PEP Boat Club competes in the annual Promoting Electric Propulsion competition hosted by the American Society of Naval Engineers (ASNE). Students build a competition racing boat and will gain engineering experience.

name: Natural Resource and Environmental Economics Club
summary: Educating and networking students interested in economic and environmental career paths.

name: Hands to Hearts Knoxville
summary: Hands to Hearts Knoxville is the UTK chapter of the Memphis-based organization that supports children with heart conditions through fundraising and awareness. All proceeds support Le Bonheur Children’s Hospital and its pediatric cardiac programs.

name: Central Asian Society
summary: Promotes Central Asian heritage and inclusion at the University of Tennessee through cultural, social, and educational events that foster understanding and cross-cultural collaboration.

name: Human Development and Family Science Student Organization
summary: Fellowship and professional development organization for Human Development and Family Science Majors/Minors at the University of Tennessee

name: Silver Wings, Lt General Frank M. Andrews Chapter
summary:
National, professional organization dedicated to creating proactive,
knowledgeable, and effective civic leaders through community service and education
about national defense. Silver Wings is Air Force ROTC-affiliated, but anybody can join.

name: Celebration Fellowship at University of Tennessee, Knoxville
summary: CF-UTK is dedicated to serving the spiritual, academic, career, and social well-being of members.

name: The Sociology Club
summary: The mission of “The Sociology Club” is to provide a safe space where students can come and discuss social events while also fostering awareness of sociological theory, and research, and its implications on modern day America and the world.

name: Risk Management Society
summary: See below

name: Undergraduate Law Review At The University of Tennessee
summary: The mission of the University of Tennessee Undergraduate Law Review is to establish an independent student-run academic journal dedicated to promoting rigorous undergraduate scholarship on law, public policy, and constitutional governance.

name: Data Science Club
summary: The purpose of the Data Science Club is to grow and connect the next generation of data science professionals. Regular meetings and events will include group activities, workshops, presentations, and opportunities for exploration and professional develop

name: Business Ethics by The Cross
summary: Business Ethics by the Cross builds an inclusive, Christ-centered community where Haslam students can explore ethical decision-making, develop as principled leaders, and learn directly from industry professionals navigating real-world ethical challenges.
